rationale: go build ./... is clean at 2da52b09. Full go test ./... shows every package green except the 2 KNOWN pre-existing failures named in the BRIEF: internal/harness TestLLM_PrecommitReexportComplete and internal/instruct TestRun_IdleNoBeads. All packages touched by spec 110 (internal/panel, internal/contextpack, internal/adr, internal/spec, internal/specgate, cmd/mindspec) pass cleanly. I checked out the merge-base 2f894401 in an absolute /tmp detached worktree (worktree add --detach /tmp/mindspec-mergebase-2f894401 2f894401) and re-ran both failing tests there: TestRun_IdleNoBeads fails identically (same 'No Active Work' vs multi-spec-active-state assertion mismatch — env-dependent on this repo's live .mindspec/state having >1 in-progress spec, matches z4ps), and TestLLM_PrecommitReexportComplete fails identically (live claude-code agent session, hook-bypass workaround reassigning core.hooksPath, non-deterministic live-LLM test unrelated to any panel/parser code). Both are confirmed pre-existing at the merge-base, not introduced by 110's diff. No regression found.

findings.detail: go build ./... clean, no errors, at 2da52b09.
go test ./... : all ok except 2 known failures. internal/harness FAIL (TestLLM_PrecommitReexportComplete, 112.31s local run); internal/instruct FAIL (TestRun_IdleNoBeads, 2.37s).

Reproduced TestRun_IdleNoBeads failure at merge-base 2f894401 in /tmp/mindspec-mergebase-2f894401 (detached worktree): identical failure — test expects idle template heading 'No Active Work' but repo's live .mindspec/state has 2 active specs (110, 111) so instruct renders the multi-spec template instead. This is a fixture/environment artifact of running the test suite inside this live multi-spec repo, not a code regression; matches tracked issue z4ps.

Reproduced TestLLM_PrecommitReexportComplete failure at merge-base 2f894401: identical failure signature (live claude-code agent invocation, ~107s, agent reassigns core.hooksPath as a hook-bypass workaround, Turn classification retry=5/forward=2, Plan fidelity 0.0%). This is a live-agent nondeterministic behavioral test unrelated to spec 110's panel/parser-parity code paths; fails the same way with or without 110's changes.
internal/panel, internal/contextpack, internal/adr, internal/spec, internal/specgate, cmd/mindspec all report ok with no failures — no regression in any package spec 110 modifies.
